title: Jedi instructor

'''Jedi instructors''' were [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] that taught the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] and [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Specializing in different fields, including politics, [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], unarmed combat, and the sciences, these Jedi came from the [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Sentinel]], [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Guardian]], and [[Jedi Consular|Consular]] branches of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Most Jedi instructors had considerable aptitude in a variety of skills that helped to maintain the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] numbers. Particularly, Jedi instructors were adept at identifying an Initiate's weak points, preventing their students from falling to the dark side, and helping young Jedi determine the role they would best fit in the Order.<ref name="TJP" />

==Overview==
Jedi instructors were stationed at every [[Jedi academy]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], assigned to oversee the rearing and the protection of the [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|Initiate clans]] to the Jedi Order. Ranging from lightsaber instructors to political theory lecturers, these Jedi Masters were experts in their field and were looked up to as paragons of wisdom amongst the Jedi. While not all instructors were a part of it, the [[Educational Corps]] served as a way to collect vast swaths of data and share it with the Order and others out in the galaxy. Specially modified starships were converted into mobile schools in the early days of the Order so that Jedi instructors could perform their lessons nearly anywhere they traveled. It was typical for these ships to vary in size and occupancy. The smallest mobile schools were contained within a small [[Transport/Legends|transport]] designed for one Jedi instructor and up to six students. The largest of these schools, such as the ''[[Chu'unthor]]'', were large enough for hundreds of Jedi trainees and dozens of instructors.<ref name="TJP" />

===Lightsaber instructors===
[[:Category:Jedi Battlemasters|''List of Battlemasters'']]; [[:Category:Lightsaber combat instructors|''List of lightsaber instructors'']]
{{Quote|Your lightsaber is an invaluable tool. Even when inactive, it can defuse a potentially volatile situation. Trust me on that.|[[Kyle Katarn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''</ref>}}
[[File:CinDrallig-SWCTP.png|thumb|250px||left|[[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]], battlemaster during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].]]
The art of lightsaber instruction was practiced by Guardians who had perfected their skills on the battlefield or on long journey-missions on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], mastering all [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|seven forms of combat]]. Not believing in the practice of teaching for life, instructors first had to demonstrate their knowledge of their art through various tests and missions. All instructors were Jedi Masters and were recommended for the role by the standing battlemaster; the chief instructor at the academy, and appointed by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. These day-to-day instructors passed on their teachings to their students, starting from [[Form I/Legends|Form I]] to [[Form VI/Legends|Form VI]] of lightsaber dueling.<ref name="TJP" />

Before the [[New Sith Wars]], there was only one '''Battlemaster''' in existence, overseeing all instruction at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. However, once the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] was called upon to defeat the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]], many [[Jedi Lord]]s and [[Jedi General/Legends|generals]] took up the mantle as they slaughtered the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] scourge. When the war ended and the Jedi were victorious, many of these Jedi revoked the title and took up teaching positions, while others were appointed to satellite training facilities where they oversaw the combat instruction while reporting to the main battlemaster on Coruscant, Master [[Skarch Vaunk]].<ref name="TJP" />

[[File:Heroes_and_Legends-HaL.jpg|thumb|260px|[[Kyle Katarn]], battlemaster of the [[New Jedi Order]].]]

While the term fell out of use following the disbanding of the Order and the death of battlemaster [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] would learn of the title and bestow it upon Jedi Master [[Kyle Katarn]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], following the [[New Jedi Order|reorganization]] of the ancient Order. While the New Order had been in its infancy, Master [[Kam Solusar]] had unofficially held the role at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], but was replaced by Master Katarn so that he could oversee the training at the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Academy]] of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]].<ref name="Jedi Academy Training Manual">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref>

===Specialized combat classes===
Focusing on [[Force power/Legends|Force talents]], there were several specialized combat classes that did not require a lightsaber at all. During the final days of the Republic, there were several instructors at the Temple who worked not only with weapons, but with the body of Initiates, turning them into living weapons. Master [[Ilena Xan]], the overseer of the [[Jedi Temple Apprentice Tournament]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], hosted a class in unarmed combat and was nicked named ''Iron Hand'' for her ability to disable an opponent with only her hands.<ref name="YDR">''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''</ref> Additional Force-based classes included the [[Art of Movement]] study, a class taught by Master [[Fy-Tor-Ana]] and held in a specialized [[Jedi Temple obstacle course|training hall]] in the Temple Ziggurat.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

===Non-Force-based classes===
[[File:JediClassroom.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An instructor lectures from the center of a large classroom.]]
While the Force was a key part of Jedi philosophy, classes at the Temple covered all subjects. Mathematics, biology, [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] theory and more were all classes held at the academy. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] Master [[Bear (Jedi)|Bear]] taught a Climbing and Ropework class that focused on knot tying and Master [[Tycho]] instructed Padawans in military strategy. Master [[Maks Leem]] taught hyperspace navigation before her death, with other instructors teaching astrocartography, comm installation tech, and botany.<ref name="YDR" />

==Behind the scenes==
It is a strong possibility that [[Anoon Bondara]] was the Jedi battlemaster prior to Cin Drallig. In both of Anoon's appearances he is described as having lightsaber skills second to none, also the fact that he was the lightsaber instructor to Younglings and Padawans such as [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and that he died in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the same year Cin Drallig became battlemaster, support this possibility. However, it is unknown whether or not he was the head lightsaber instructor at the Temple and therefore the battlemaster.
